description:
Offered: every other year
This course explores lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) issues in society and literature. We examine the historical and current construction of gender and identity. Students interpret fiction, nonfiction, and poetry using multiple perspectives; additionally, they interpret contemporary cultural texts, such as speech, art, advertising, and film.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, DP, LI
